title: Real-World A/B Test: Genome Context vs No Context
hypothesis: Genome context improves implementation quality on complex multi-file tasks

methodology.task: Add collar hedge strategy (protective put + covered call) across 7+ files with Black-Scholes pricing, Section 1256 tax treatment, Monte Carlo integration, VaR impact, and UI components

conditions.A: WITH genome CLAUDE.md (~1913 tokens, 117 lines, 14x compression)
conditions.B: WITHOUT genome (no CLAUDE.md, must explore codebase from scratch)

methodology.genome_source: Auto-generated via gemini CLI from README.md + CONTRIBUTING.md + METHODOLOGY.md + package.json + requirements.txt (70.8KB total)

where_genome_helped: Found legacy InvestmentCalculator.jsx view that B missed — genome listed it as 'Primary UI/Dashboard'
Triggered ESLint verification — awareness of project linting setup
More complete prop threading due to genome mapping component hierarchy
where_genome_did_not_help: Core math quality identical (BS pricing, skew adjustments, NDX scaling)
Tax treatment identical (Section 1256 blended rate, short-term penalty)
B slightly better on Monte Carlo and VaR integration
Genome caused 2.3x more token usage (agent cross-referenced notation verbosely)

post_test_architecture_analysis.initial_slop_diagnosis: Both agents were initially suspected of adding redundant collar clamping in monteCarloSimulation.js and riskMetrics.js
post_test_architecture_analysis.actual_finding: Architecture has 3 independent simulation engines: (1) deterministic year-by-year in orchestrator, (2) GBM Monte Carlo in monteCarloSimulation.js, (3) block-bootstrap VaR paths in orchestrator's simulateLeveragedPath. All three legitimately need collar clamping.
post_test_architecture_analysis.real_dry_violation: Collar preset values hardcoded in computeCollarBounds() instead of importing from COLLAR_CONFIG constants
post_test_architecture_analysis.lesson: Verify architecture before diagnosing slop. Independent simulation engines are not redundant logic.

conclusion: Marginal quality edge (+2 points, 92 vs 90) at 2.3x token cost. Genome's value is cross-cutting awareness (non-obvious files to touch), not implementation quality. Value proposition likely scales with codebase size and number of scattered context files.
